title: Human Writing and Machine Encoding
subtitle: Authorship, serialization, and the layers of signed text
summary: Native anchor draft for the Writing and Representation in Sutton SignWriting series on the distinction between human signed writing and machine encoding.
synopsis: This entry argues that SignWriting is first a human writing system and only secondarily a machine-encoded text format. It distinguishes written signs, symbol inventories, signboxes, encodings, rendering, styling, layout, search, and analysis; uses an FSW/SWU example to show the layer relationship; and explains why serialization does not make plane-based writing into line-based writing.
